Note.Para: Galley chiller capability may be degraded. Check chiller status on Cabin Attendant Panel or local galley panels.

ListItem.Para: If cabin temperatures become warmer than desired, consider one or more of the following temporary actions:
Select IFE system modes designed to reduce cabin heat loads.

Note.Para: Availability of modes dependent on IFE system installed and airline modifiable options.

ListItem.Para: Set IFE off using the IFE Power switch on the Video Control Station.
Select HEAT REDUCTION mode using the Cabin Attendant Panel TEMPERATURE menu (adjusts cabin lighting and window shading).

ListItem.rev:revHighlight: Update (O) note to provide additional guidance to address warm temperature in cabin
ListItem.Para: Set Upper Recirc Fans OFF on the P5 overhead panel

ListItem.rev:revHighlight: Update (O) note to provide additional guidance to address warm temperature in cabin

Note.Para: The RECIRC FAN UPR OFF advisory message will be displayed

ListItem.rev:revHighlight: Update (O) note to provide additional guidance to address warm temperature in cabin
ListItem.Para: If the cabin temperatures become warmer after the TRIM AIR is selected OFF, TRIM AIR can be selected to ON
If on ground, set FCAC OFF.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: Integrated cooling system may be inoperative deactivated.

Note.Para: INTEGRATED CLG SYSTEM status message may be displayed.
INTEGRATED CLG RECIRC AIR status message will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
Gain access to the following ICS components:
Disconnect, cap and stow the electrical connectors to the following ICS components:
Replace recirculation filters if exposed to fluid spray.

Note.Para: When dispatching with the Integrated Cooling System deactivated, galley chiller capability will be lost

ListItem.Para: If cabin temperatures become warmer than desired, consider one or more of the following temporary actions:
Select IFE system modes designed to reduce cabin heat loads.

Note.Para: Availability of modes dependent on IFE system installed and airline modifiable options.

ListItem.Para: Set IFE off using the IFE Power switch on the Video Control Station.
Select HEAT REDUCTION mode using the Cabin Attendant Panel TEMPERATURE menu (adjusts cabin lighting and window shading).

ListItem.rev:revHighlight: Update (O) note to provide additional guidance to address warm temperature in cabin
ListItem.Para: Set Upper Recirc Fans OFF on the P5 overhead panel

ListItem.rev:revHighlight: Update (O) note to provide additional guidance to address warm temperature in cabin

Note.Para: The RECIRC FAN UPR OFF advisory message will be displayed

ListItem.rev:revHighlight: Update (O) note to provide additional guidance to address warm temperature in cabin
ListItem.Para: If the cabin temperatures become warmer after the TRIM AIR is selected OFF, TRIM AIR can be selected to ON

Effectivity.rev:revHighlight: Update (O) note to provide additional guidance to address warm temperature in cabin

ListItem.Para: If on ground, set FCAC OFF.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: Individual components may be inoperative provided leaking components are isolated.

Note.Para: Any portion of system that operates normally may be used.

The following procedure is only used for ICS components that are leaking fluid. For components that are not leaking, ICS will usually operate normally with no action required. However, inoperative components can be shut off by opening the associated circuit breaker.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate leaking supplemental cooling unit (SCU) or galley cooling unit (GCU) as required (AMM DDG 21-53-01).

ListItem.Para: Deactivate leaking SCU. An inoperative unit may be removed provided dispatch tool K21018 is installed. SCUs are interchangeable.
For SCU 1, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open ICS COMPRESSOR-SCU 1 circuit breaker and lock with an INOP tag.
For SCU 2, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open ICS COMPRESSOR-SCU 2 circuit breaker and lock with an INOP tag.
For SCU 3, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open ICS COMPRESSOR-SCU 3 circuit breaker and lock with an INOP tag.
For SCU 4, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open ICS COMPRESSOR-SCU 4 circuit breaker and lock with an INOP tag.
Deactivate leaking GCU. An inoperative galley cooling unit may be removed.
For the GCU on galley 1A, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For the GCU on galley 1F, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For the GCU on galley 2A, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For the GCU on galley 2F, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For the GCU on galley 2M, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For the GCU on galley 3A, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For the GCU on galley 3F, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For the GCU on galley 4C, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For the GCU on galley 4L, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
For the GCU on galley 4R, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
Disconnect supply and return hoses from the leaking GCU.
Install hose plug assemblies (K21018-6) in GCU supply and return quick disconnects.
Stow disconnected supply and return hoses.
